---
title: "Attention and Context Economy"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Chapter under construction (v2 draft)

This chapter is being authored in Wave B of the Practitioner Block v2 build. The brief lives in `WIP-distillation/round-3/04-FINAL-ARC-PROPOSAL.md`, section 5.
:::

## Thesis

Context is finite. Attention degrades non-linearly under load. Doubling input length does not halve output quality; it crosses a threshold past which the model loses the thread. This is *physics*, not preference, and the disciplines that compensate are not optional polish.

## The Monday-morning failure

A team's reliable code-review prompt starts producing inconsistent results. No one changed the prompt. They added a "useful" 800-line context file two weeks ago. The model's context window can fit the new content; the model's *attention* cannot. The cure is not a bigger model; it is progressive disclosure of the new content.

## Sections (to be authored)

1. Why context is not a budget; it is a working set.
2. Plan-write-then-reload.
3. The bounded-scope rule for external grounding.
4. Signal-to-noise as a design metric.
5. The PROSE constraints, revisited.
